item.title: Grocery, Farm, and Food Worker Stabilization Grant Program Act of 2026

latestAction.text: Referred to the House Committee on Agriculture.

item.text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.

summary.text:
Grocery, Farm, and Food Worker Protection Act of 2026

This bill directs the Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) to establish a grant program to provide farmworkers, meat processing workers, and grocery workers stabilization payments in the case of a natural disaster or other disaster. Specifically, AMS must provide funding to membership organizations or labor unions representing farmworkers, meat processing workers, and grocery workers for the payments.

bill.title: Grocery, Farm, and Food Worker Protection Act of 2026

item.title: Grocery, Farm, and Food Worker Protection Act of 2026
Grocery, Farm, and Food Worker Protection Act of 2026
A bill to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a grocery, farm, and food worker stabilization grant program.

latestAction.text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.

dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.
dublinCore.dc:contributor: Congressional Research Service, Library of Congress
dublinCore.dc:description: This file contains bill summaries and statuses for federal legislation. A bill summary describes the most significant provisions of a piece of legislation and details the effects the legislative text may have on current law and federal programs. Bill summaries are authored by the Congressional Research Service (CRS) of the Library of Congress. As stated in Public Law 91-510 (2 USC 166 (d)(6)), one of the duties of CRS is "to prepare summaries and digests of bills and resolutions of a public general nature introduced in the Senate or House of Representatives". For more information, refer to the User Guide that accompanies this file.
